data.title: Stanley Black & Decker declares $0.82 dividend
data.description:
• Stanley Black & Decker (NYSE:SWK) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.77%

  • See SWK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bel Fuse Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.62, revenue of $149.86M beats by $23.45M
data.description:
• Bel Fuse press release (NASDAQ:BELFB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.62.
  • Revenue of $149.86M (+7.0% Y/Y) beats by $23.45M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $30.3 million (20.2% of sales) as compared to $27.3 million (19.5% of sales) in Q4-23
  • Completed acquisition of Enercon, making aerospace and defense Bel's largest end market served.

data.title: Elon Musk, DOGE score victory against state AGs seeking to block access
data.description:
A federal judge in Washington on Tuesday denied a request from 14 state attorneys general to stop the Department of Government Efficiency and Elon Musk from accessing data at seven federal agencies.

Judge Tanya Chutkan wrote that the plaintiffs "legitimately call into question what appears to be the unchecked authority of an unelected individual and an entity that was not created by Congress."

And while Congress lacks oversight over DOGE, she said she is unable to issue a temporary restraining order "without clear evidence of imminent, irreparable harm, to these Plaintiffs. The current record does not meet that standard."

The state AGs are seeking to block DOGE and Musk, who is CEO of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), from accessing information at the Office of Personnel Management, the Department of Education, the Department of Labor, the Department of Health and Human Services, the Department of Energy, the Department of Transportation, and the Department of Commerce, as well as preventing DOGE from firing or putting on involuntary leave employees at the agencies.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Sandstorm Gold reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Sandstorm Gold press release (NYSE:SAND): Q4 Revenue of $47.4M (+6.5% Y/Y) beats by $0.17M.
  • Net income of $3.1M.
  • Annual production of 72,810 attributable gold equivalent ounces1 including 17,721 ounces in the fourth quarter (compared to 97,245 and 23,250 ounces for the comparable periods in 2023).
  • Annual cash flows from operating activities, excluding changes in non-cash working capital1 of $139.0 million including $36.8 million in the fourth quarter (compared to $151.1 million and $36.5 million for the comparable periods in 2023);
  • Record cash operating margins1 of $2,097 per attributable gold equivalent ounce for the full year and $2,396 per ounce for the fourth quarter (compared to $1,706 and $1,737 per ounce for the comparable periods in 2023).
  • 2025 Production Guidance and Updated Long-term Outlook: Based on the Company's existing streams and royalties, attributable gold equivalent ounces are forecasted to be between 65,000 and 80,000 ounces in 2025, which considers a range of commodity price scenarios, a phased ramp-up at Equinox Gold's Greenstone mine, and the expected removal of fixed deliveries from the Vatukoula mine. With recent advancements within Sandstorm's development portfolio, the Company is increasing its long-term production forecast to approximately 150,000 attributable gold equivalent ounces in 2030, based on the Company's existing streams and royalties plus the exercise of the Company's exclusive gold stream option on the MARA project in Argentina.

data.title: Largo appoints Gordon Babcock and Luis Rendón as co-COOs
data.description:
• Largo (NASDAQ:LGO) on Tuesday said it has appointed Gordon Babcock and Luis Rendón as Co-Chief Operating Officers, following the resignation of former COO, Celio Pereira for personal reasons.
  • "The Co-COO structure is intended to enhance oversight of the company’s operations, with each executive assuming responsibility for distinct operational areas to improve efficiencies," the company said.
  • LGO +1.27% after hours to $1.7621.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Société BIC SA reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Société BIC SA press release (OTCPK:BICEY): Q4 net sales of €517.5 million.
  • Adjusted EPS of €1.40.
  • Adjusted EBIT of €70.7 million.
  • 2025 Outlook: the Group expects net sales growth between +4% and +6% at constant currency, adjusted EBIT margin at the same level as 2024, at 15.6%, above BIC’s Horizon target, and free cash flow above €240 million.

data.title: HP plans to acquire Humane, but not its AI pin: report
data.description:
HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) plans to acquire the Silicon Valley startup Humane, but the deal does not include its wearable artificial intelligence-powered clip on computer, known as the Ai Pin, according to Bloomberg.

HP is acquiring Humane's software platform and intellectual property for $116M, according to the report. HP also plans to retain most of its employees. However, Humane's Ai pin business is coming to an end. The Humane team is expected to form a new division at HP with the goal of implementing AI tech in HP's products.

The Ai pin was released in November 2023. The device, meant to be worn on clothing, could be tapped to talk to a virtual assistant and beam text and images on a user's palm through a laser projection system. However, the wearable computer, which cost $699 and a $24 per month subscription, racked up numerous bad reviews and was poorly received.

However, the technology behind the pin was considered ahead of its time, and could be implemented into other products, which is what HP reportedly plans to do.

data.title: EVS Broadcast Equipment SA reports FY results
data.description:
• EVS Broadcast Equipment SA press release (OTCPK:EVSBY): FY net profit of  €42.9 million.
